title: aster hyssopifolius var. hirtus
intro: Aster hyssopifolius var. hirtus, also known as the hairy mountain aster, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is primarily found in mountainous regions and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities. The hairy mountain aster has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as coughs, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to ease congestion and soothe irritated tissues. It can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of illness.
